title: Bound protocol event and response serialization before allocation
description: Resource-bound finding from bd-62e2c5. ConnectionWriter.send in src/server.ts calls encodeLine and Buffer.from on the complete response or SDK event before checking maxOutboundBytesPerConnection. Model message events are not journal-record bounded, and mapped Pi event payloads can contain full messages, tool results, or provider error data. Therefore the advertised outbound bound controls only queued bytes after an unbounded JSON serialization and allocation. Define per-event and per-response payload limits, use bounded serialization or deliberate truncation and typed overflow behavior, prevent non-serializable SDK payloads from disrupting session delivery, and add adversarial tests proving a large event cannot grow process memory or affect other sessions.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Audit Cacophony integration gaps for standalone Pi Daemon
description: Completed coordinated Cacophony-side consumer audit for Harry's 2026-07-14 Pi Daemon review. Current Cacophony main has standalone host config/preference, sidecar supervision, an exec wrapper, a status endpoint, and a bounded low-level UDS client, but no pico-daemon agent type or create/message/attach/health/lifecycle wiring. Findings sent to bd-62e2c5 owner include explicit atomic snapshot-plus-stream attach, durable CRUD for dormant sessions, async message acceptance and request lookup, full AgentSessionRuntime/RPC control parity, neutral whole-service bearer auth, REST and NDJSON equivalence, APC/ACP adapter work, and a neutral stock-Pi or pico-style attach client. Cacophony-only integration remains outside this repository.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-2

title: Add bearer-authenticated API transport and explicit session attach
description: Release-blocking access-control and event-routing finding from bd-62e2c5, narrowed by operator direction on 2026-07-14. The first full API uses a configurable bind address and one server-wide bearer token; all authenticated callers share one service trust domain, while finer-grained per-session authorization is deferred. Current owner-only Unix NDJSON has no bearer auth, handshake exposes resident sessions, and any successful or active session-scoped request implicitly subscribes its connection to future model events. Add the configured authenticated API listener, secret-safe token loading and comparison, fail-closed startup, explicit generation-bound attach and detach, and tests proving unauthenticated requests are denied and status or failed commands never create subscriptions. Keep Cacophony bearer and PKI concepts out of the standalone protocol.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Audit standalone Pi Daemon completion surface
description: Coordinated operator-requested audit. Own the standalone Pi Daemon core, protocol, durability, isolation, security, tests, and documentation gap review. Incorporate path/line findings from bd-1245ab covering the Cacophony consumer/deployment and packaging/release surface. Update PLAN.md with evidence-backed remaining work, register actionable implementation beads in dependency order, and distinguish completed behavior, Cacophony-only integration, release operations, and true Pi Daemon gaps.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Make automated npm dependency updates Nix-aware
description: Dependabot PR 6 updates package-lock.json but cannot refresh flake.nix npmDepsHash, so both Nix jobs fail with a fixed-output hash mismatch. The same grouped update exposes stricter Node 26 socket data callback types in src/client.ts and src/server.ts. Add a supported maintenance path that refreshes the pinned Nix hash, adapts the socket chunk typing without weakening runtime bounds, and leaves grouped dependency PRs with actionable CI rather than guaranteed red Nix checks. Discovered during the post-v0.1.0 release-candidate health audit on 2026-07-14.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Restrict GitHub CI to self-hosted runners
description:
Configure GitHub Actions CI workflows to run exclusively on self-hosted runners, matching the pattern used by the cacophony project and other caco projects.

Acceptance criteria:
- All CI workflow jobs use `runs-on` targeting the self-hosted runner label(s) rather than GitHub-hosted runners (e.g. ubuntu-latest).
- Configuration is consistent with how cacophony and other caco projects define their self-hosted runners.
- CI runs successfully on the self-hosted infrastructure after the change.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

bead.title: Deliver the full standalone Pi session host API
bead.description: Parent delivery epic established by the 2026-07-14 completion audit. Evolve the v0.1 no-tools NDJSON scaffold into a standalone Cacophony-independent Pi daemon with durable session CRUD by ID or name, the existing neutral NDJSON control mode, an additive bearer-authenticated JSON API, multi-reader Pi-RPC-compatible attachment, ACP translation at the requested apc path, full supported Pi AgentSessionRuntime features and per-session configuration, a remote attachment client, explicit trust and isolation modes, bounded operations, and releaseable packaging. Cacophony consumption is downstream and must not become a dependency.
bead.creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

bead.title: Specify additive session CRUD, RPC attach, and ACP API contracts
bead.description: Define the versioned contract before implementation. Preserve existing Unix NDJSON commands as one control mode and add authenticated JSON endpoints for session collection and session by ID or name CRUD, rpc attachment, and the operator-requested apc path implementing upstream ACP. Specify request and event envelopes, HTTP or local transport mapping, error/status codes, session aliases and generations, pagination, capabilities, request tickets, attach cursors, snapshot/live atomicity, replay gaps, reconnect and host identity, multi-reader response routing, extension UI controller semantics, and equivalence rules between control modes. Publish JSON Schema plus OpenAPI or equivalent fixtures and compatibility tests. Child of the full standalone host epic.
bead.creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

Publish JSON Schema plus OpenAPI or equivalent fixtures and compatibility tests. Child of the full standalone host epic.

bead.title: Implement a durable resident and dormant session catalog with CRUD
bead.description: Current host status and close operate only on resident SessionSlot objects; idle eviction makes retained sessions invisible and undeletable. Build a bounded durable catalog keyed by immutable ID with optional unique name, generation, cwd, Pi session target, runtime configuration digest, isolation mode, resident or dormant state, timestamps, and last terminal state. Implement create, list, get, mutable update where safe, reopen, close, and delete for both resident and dormant sessions with pagination and crash-safe metadata. Emit explicit dormant, evicted, reopened, renamed, and deleted lifecycle events. Keep catalog data neutral and exclude Cacophony objects.
bead.creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

bead.title: Preserve Pi conversation identity across restart and runtime replacement
bead.description: Release-blocking persistence defect found by bd-62e2c5. Durable manifests currently restore session mode new by calling SessionManager.create again and restore memory mode as an empty in-memory session, so a logical session can lose its Pi conversation history across daemon restart while queued wakes replay into the wrong context. Move each slot to AgentSessionRuntime, persist the actual active Pi session file and replacement metadata, reject or clearly mark ephemeral modes, rebind events and extensions after new, switch, fork, clone, or import, and add real SDK restart and dormant-reopen tests proving conversation identity and entry continuity.
bead.creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

bead.title: Add asynchronous durable command tickets and request reconciliation
bead.description: Decouple admission from model-turn completion. Prompt or wake should durably acknowledge queued or accepted work immediately and return a ticket, while attach and request-status APIs expose progress, terminal result, failure, or indeterminate state. Add bounded lookup by idempotency key or ticket, terminal-result retrieval, explicit reconciliation using Pi session entries after restart, and retention semantics. Journal and deduplicate steer, follow-up, extension command, and other mutating RPC controls or explicitly classify operations that cannot be safely retried. Keep an optional wait-for-terminal convenience without making it the only admission path.
bead.creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

bead.title: Host the full supported Pi RPC surface on AgentSessionRuntime
bead.description: Replace the narrow prompt, steer, followUp, abort adapter with an in-process AgentSessionRuntime host compatible with stock Pi RPC semantics. Cover prompt and images, queue modes, state and messages, model and max thinking control, compaction and retry, session stats, commands, entries and tree cursors, naming, new, switch, fork, clone, import and export, bash where policy permits, extension UI request and response, agent_settled, entry_appended, and session replacement rebinding. Maintain typed capability discovery and conformance fixtures against the pinned Pi RPC protocol rather than importing Cacophony types.
bead.creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

bead.title: Implement explicit multi-reader attach with snapshot, replay, and gaps
bead.description: Provide session rpc attachment over the additive API and a corresponding explicit attach and detach operation for NDJSON. Attach must atomically return the current session and request snapshot plus a live event boundary; support multiple bounded readers, origin-routed command responses, event sequence cursors, bounded replay after sequence, an explicit gap requiring resync, host restart identity, ping or keepalive, typed detach versus session exit, and reconnect. Define one-controller or first-response semantics for extension UI dialogs while allowing many observers. Add two-client attach, disconnect, reconnect, slow-reader, host-restart, and session-replacement acceptance tests.
bead.creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

bead.title: Support Pi CLI-equivalent per-session runtime configuration
bead.description: POST session creation must accept a typed equivalent of supported Pi CLI startup policy: cwd and session target, name, provider and model, thinking including max, scoped models, tools and exclusions, system and appended prompts, context files, extensions, skills, prompt templates, packages, settings overrides, project trust or approve policy, offline controls, images, and a bounded environment overlay. Default isolation mode is explicitly unisolated in-process. Never mutate process.env per turn; provider credentials can use session-scoped AuthStorage data and tool environment can use scoped operations or spawn hooks. Document that arbitrary extensions share the daemon process and require trusted clients; reserve future container, VM, or tool-routing backends behind an additive isolation capability.
bead.creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

bead.title: Expose an in-process ACP adapter at the session apc endpoint
bead.description: Implement the operator-requested session apc endpoint while documenting that the upstream protocol and adapter are Agent Client Protocol, ACP, and pi-acp. Reuse or port the MIT pi-acp translation layer against each in-process AgentSessionRuntime instead of spawning pi --mode rpc. Cover ACP JSON-RPC routing, initialize and capabilities, session list or load or prompt, tools, permissions and client requests, commands and streaming updates, cancellation, multi-client behavior, bearer auth, durable session identity, and lifecycle parity with NDJSON and rpc attachment. Do not add Cacophony dependencies.
bead.creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

bead.title: Ship a standalone remote attach client and Pi connect extension
bead.description: Provide an operator-facing way to control a daemon session as though it were local. Prefer a standalone Pi package extension that registers connect with a session ID or name, intercepts local input, enters remote or slave mode, forwards Pi-RPC commands and extension UI responses, renders streamed events and transcript state, shows reconnect or gap status, and cleans up on session shutdown. If stock Pi lacks a safe remote-runtime seam, ship a neutral pico-style attach binary or TUI and document the limitation; do not import caco-picophony. Add install and interactive protocol tests.
bead.creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0
